data.title: IPW: Q2 2026 revenue dropped 60%, but gross margin stayed strong and costs fell after restructuring
data.description:
Revenue fell to $7.1M in Q2 2026 due to a strategic supply chain shift, but gross margin held at 44%. Operating expenses dropped 28% year-over-year, and a major cost center was divested post-quarter, positioning for improved resilience and future growth.

Original document: iPower Inc. [IPW] SEC 8-K Current Report — Feb. 20 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste SAB de CV expected to post earnings of MXN9.91 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste SAB de CV is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on February 24 for the period ending December 31 2025

  • The Cuajimalpa Mexico, D.f.-based company is expected to report a 6.4% increase in revenue to MXN9.594 billion from MXN9.02 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 7 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste SAB de CV is for earnings of MXN9.91 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 9 "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste SAB de CV is MXN655.05, about 1.9% above its last closing price of MXN642.68

data.title: TSX adds to weekly gain as investors cheer US tariff ruling
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSX ends up 0.7% at 33,817.51
  • For the week, the index gains 2.25%
  • Materials group adds 1.7% as metal prices rise
  • Superior Plus tumbles 18.4% after revenue miss

By Fergal Smith

Canada's main stock index rose to another record high on Friday, helped by gains for financial and metal mining shares, as investors welcomed the U.S. Supreme Court's ruling against President Donald Trump's tariffs.

The S&P/TSX Composite Index ended up 222.53 points, or 0.7%, at 33,817.51, eclipsing Thursday's record closing high. For the week, the index advanced 2.25%.

Trump responded with fury to the Supreme Court ruling that he did not have the power to unilaterally set tariffs on imports, denigrating individual justices as he vowed to wield a more restrictive law to continue his global trade war.

"That's a crack in the armor... There are limits to what Trump can do," said Colin Cieszynski, chief market strategist at SIA Wealth Management.

"That's certainly being taken by traders in Canada as a positive."

Canada sends about 70% of its exports to the United States so its economy has been badly hurt by the trade war.

Domestic data on Friday was mixed. Retail sales fell 0.4% in December but a preliminary estimate showed a rebound of 1.5% in January.

The materials sector , which includes metal mining shares, rose 1.7% as gold and copper prices climbed.

Technology added 1.4% and financials (.SPTTFS), which included heavily weighted bank stocks, were up 0.9%.

"Anything that reduces political tensions between the United States and Canada would be positive for the banks given the amount of cross-border operations they've got going on," Cieszynski said.

Half of the 10 major sectors posted declines, including consumer staples (.GSPTTCS). It lost 1.3% and energy ended 1.2% lower.

U.S. crude oil futures settled 0.1% lower at $66.39 a barrel as the market awaited developments in the tense relationship between the U.S. and Iran.

Shares of energy distribution firm Superior Plus Corp tumbled 18.4% after the company missed quarterly revenue estimates.

data.title: 'I'm ashamed': Trump denounces Supreme Court justices after tariffs ruling
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump blasts two justices he picked, Barrett and Gorsuch
  • They are "an embarrassment to their families," Trump says
  • Trump alleges court is "swayed by foreign interests"
  • President offers no evidence for his allegation

By John Kruzel

President Donald Trump lashed out on Friday at the U.S. Supreme Court and the six justices who struck down his signature global tariffs - including two he appointed - in remarkably personal terms while hailing the three justices who backed him.

Although previous presidents have sharply criticized Supreme Court rulings against them, Trump's lengthy tirade to reporters at the White House stood out for its contemptuous tone, as well as the personal nature of his scorn and praise.

It represented a remarkable attack by a sitting president on the top U.S. judicial body, even after Trump and other senior officials in his administration over the past year have assailed lower court judges for rulings impeding his policies since he returned to office in January 2025.

The Supreme Court, which has a 6-3 conservative majority, had over the past year permitted Trump's expansive assertion of executive power, with few exceptions, in a series of decisions issued on an emergency basis.

But Friday's ruling delivered Trump a stinging defeat. It was authored by Chief Justice John Roberts and joined by two fellow conservatives who Trump appointed during his first term in office - Neil Gorsuch and Amy Coney Barrett - as well as the three liberal justices.

"The Supreme Court's ruling on tariffs is deeply disappointing. And I'm ashamed of certain members of the court - absolutely ashamed - for not having the courage to do what's right for our country," Trump said at the opening of remarks lasting 45 minutes.

Speaking to reporters from behind a dimly lit lectern in the White House briefing room, Trump impugned the patriotism of some of the justices and claimed that the court "has been swayed by foreign interests," but declined to provide any evidence.

And he heaped scorn on Gorsuch and Barrett for joining the 6-3 majority ruling that sank his tariffs - a favorite foreign policy cudgel - imposed under a law meant for national emergencies.

"I think it's an embarrassment to their families, you wanna know the truth, the two of them," Trump said, referring to Gorsuch and Barrett.

Trump spoke glowingly of a dissenting opinion written on Friday by his other court appointee, Justice Brett Kavanaugh, that was joined by fellow conservatives Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito.

"I'd like to thank and congratulate Justices Thomas, Alito, and Kavanaugh for their strength and wisdom and love of our country, which is, right now, very proud of those justices," Trump said. "When you read the dissenting opinions, there's no way that anyone can argue against them."

Kavanaugh wrote that the court's ruling against Trump did not necessarily foreclose the president "from imposing most if not all of these same sorts of tariffs under other statutory authorities," adding that "the court's decision is not likely to greatly restrict presidential tariff authority going forward."

"In essence, the court today concludes that the president checked the wrong statutory box by relying on (an emergency economic powers law) rather than another statute to impose these tariffs," Kavanaugh wrote.

Trump repeatedly mentioned Kavanaugh, who he appointed to a lifetime position on the court in 2018 and stuck with him through a bruising Senate confirmation battle in which the nominee denied allegations of sexual assault dating back decades.

"I would like to thank Justice Kavanaugh for his, frankly, his genius and his great ability," Trump said. "Very proud of that appointment."

Trump called the three liberal justices "the Democrats on the court."

"They're an automatic no, just like in Congress. They're an automatic no. They're against anything that makes America strong, healthy and great again," Trump said. "They also are a, frankly, disgrace to our nation, those justices."

data.title: Regional Management Corp. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Regional Management Corp., a prominent consumer finance company specializing in large and small installment loans, has released its 2025 10-K report. The report highlights the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a dynamic market environment.

Financial Highlights

Regional Management Corp. reported robust financial performance for 2025, marked by significant growth across key metrics:

  • Total Revenue: $645.6 million, a 9.7% increase from 2024, driven by higher interest and fee income.
  • Net Income: $44.4 million, up 7.7% from 2024, attributed to increased revenue and controlled expense growth.
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic $4.71, Diluted $4.45, reflecting the rise in net income.
  • Interest and Fee Income: $578.9 million, a 9.5% increase from 2024, primarily due to a 10.3% rise in average net finance receivables.
  • Insurance Income, Net: $45.6 million, up 12.0% from 2024, with personal property insurance premiums as the largest component.
  • Other Income: $21.1 million, an 11.4% increase from 2024, mainly due to higher sales of club membership products.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $245.4 million, a 15.7% increase from 2024, due to higher net credit losses and provision expense.
  • Total General and Administrative Expenses: $257.6 million, a 4.0% increase from 2024, with personnel expenses as the largest component.
  • Interest Expense: $84.8 million, a 13.8% increase from 2024, due to an increase in the average balance of debt facilities.

Business Highlights

Regional Management Corp. demonstrated strong operational performance in 2025, with key business activities and expansions:

  • Revenue Segments: The company offers large and small installment loans, with large loans contributing significantly to finance receivables. As of December 31, 2025, large loans represented $1.6 billion in finance receivables, while small loans accounted for $547.0 million.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in 19 states, with Texas, North Carolina, and South Carolina being key markets for large loans. Texas alone accounted for 31% of large loan finance receivables. For small loans, Texas also led with 25% of the portfolio.
  • Sales Units: As of December 31, 2025, the company had 289,300 large loans and 301,500 small loans outstanding. The average size of large loans was approximately $5,500, while small loans averaged around $1,800.
  • New Product Launches: The company introduced an enhanced auto-secured large loan product, which contributed to 13.7% of the total portfolio by the end of 2025.
  • Branch Network Expansion: The company expanded its branch network to 353 locations across 19 states, with a focus on a lighter branch footprint strategy to service wider geographic areas more efficiently.
  • Digital Capabilities: The company enhanced its digital offerings by rolling out an improved digital prequalification experience, launching a customer portal, and deploying a mobile app, maintaining digital volumes at nearly 32% of total new customer volumes in 2025.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding its geographic presence, leveraging direct mail marketing, and improving digital capabilities to attract and serve customers. It also aims to enhance its product offerings and maintain sound underwriting and credit control.

Strategic Initiatives

Regional Management Corp. is focused on several strategic initiatives to drive long-term growth and shareholder value:

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is executing a strategy to grow its higher-margin small loan portfolio and high-quality auto-secured loan portfolio. It has expanded its branch network by opening 17 new branches and is optimizing its branch footprint to enhance service delivery. The company is also investing in digital and technological capabilities, including a new front-end branch origination platform, to support its omni-channel strategy.
  • Capital Management: The company maintains a strong liquidity profile with $149.2 million of available liquidity and $511.4 million of unused capacity on revolving credit facilities. It has diversified its funding sources through revolving credit facilities and securitizations. The company declared and paid $11.5 million in dividends in 2025 and repurchased 0.8 million shares of common stock at a total cost of $27.7 million. The Board authorized a $60 million stock repurchase program, effective through June 30, 2027.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its growth strategy by expanding its loan portfolio, particularly in higher-margin segments, while maintaining a strong liquidity position. It will focus on managing credit risk and optimizing its branch network. The company aims to sustain its dividend payments and continue its stock repurchase program, subject to market conditions and Board discretion.

Challenges and Risks

Regional Management Corp. faces several challenges and risks in its operations, which it is actively managing:

  • Operational and Market Risks: The company operates in a rapidly changing environment with several risks, including managing growth effectively, exposure to credit and repayment risks, and changes in the competitive environment. The geographic concentration of the loan portfolio and reliance on third-party service providers also pose significant risks. Additionally, the company faces challenges related to technological improvements, security breaches, and the development and use of AI.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is exposed to regulatory risks, including changes in laws or regulations and the impact of changes in tax laws. Ownership of common stock is subject to market volatility and anti-takeover provisions.
  • Credit and Economic Risks: The company is exposed to credit risk, particularly as a significant portion of its borrowers are non-prime borrowers. Social and economic factors, such as unemployment and inflation, may affect loan repayment. The company’s insurance operations are subject to changes in laws and borrower demand, which could impact financial results.
  • Interest Rate Risks: The company is subject to interest rate risk, which could affect its results of operations and financial condition. Elevated interest rates increase the cost of capital, influencing the interest paid on credit facilities. The company also faces risks related to its securitizations, which may expose it to financing and other risks. There is no assurance that the company will be able to access the securitization market in the future, which may require seeking more costly financing.
  • Management Strategies: Management is focused on strategic responses to these challenges, including enhancing technological capabilities and maintaining a strong compliance framework.

SEC Filing: Regional Management Corp. [ RM ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Evolution Metals & Technologies Corp. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Evolution Metals & Technologies Corp. (EM&T), a fully integrated critical materials and technology company, has released its Form 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The company focuses on building a secure, reliable, and self-sustaining U.S.-aligned supply chain for critical minerals and materials, primarily through the recycling of end-of-life materials.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Loss: $1.79 million, primarily due to operating expenses and taxes, partially offset by interest income.
  • Net Loss Per Share of Common Stock - Redemption Feature: $(0.59), reflecting the loss per share for shares with redemption rights.
  • Net Loss Per Share of Common Stock - No Redemption Feature: $(0.59), reflecting the loss per share for shares without redemption rights.

Business Highlights

  • Business Model and Operations: EM&T is focused on building a secure, reliable, and self-sustaining U.S.-aligned supply chain for critical minerals and materials, including rare earth elements, primarily through the recycling of end-of-life materials.
  • Operational Segments: The company operates across several midstream to downstream segments, including feedstock processing, oxide production, metal and alloy manufacturing, powder production, and the manufacture of bonded and sintered rare earth magnets.
  • Vertically Integrated Platform: EM&T employs a closed-loop recycling model, converting waste streams into high-value inputs for downstream manufacturing, including the recovery of base and precious metals from U.S. government-classified and commercial end-of-life electronic waste.
  • U.S. Industrial Campus Strategy: The company is developing a large-scale, integrated industrial campus in the United States to co-locate recycling, refining, and manufacturing operations across the rare earth magnet and battery materials value chain.
  • Hydrometallurgical Processing: EM&T plans to establish a large-scale hydrometallurgical processing facility in the U.S. to process recycled feedstock from magnets, batteries, and electronic scrap.
  • Technology and Automation: The company integrates proprietary AI and smart machine technologies to automate key production and material-handling functions.
  • Market and End-Use Applications: EM&T supplies critical materials to customers in the automotive, renewable energy, consumer electronics, and defense sectors.
  • Growth Objectives: The company aims to develop significant rare earth magnet manufacturing capacity and battery-grade materials production in the U.S. within two years.
  • Replication Strategy: EM&T intends to replicate and expand its existing rare earth magnet production facilities from South Korea to the U.S.
  • Three-Pronged Operating Platform: The company operates an integrated platform spanning rare earth magnet manufacturing, lithium-ion battery materials recycling, and U.S. government and commercial e-waste processing.
  • Feedstock Strategy: The company plans to source a significant portion of its rare earth feedstock from electronic scrap, including classified and unclassified materials generated by U.S. government agencies and commercial markets.
  • Management and Engineering Capabilities: EM&T's operations are led by an experienced management and engineering team with extensive expertise across the rare earth magnet and critical materials value chain.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on executing its post-combination operating plan and capital formation strategy, with plans to conduct a primary equity offering to raise additional capital for its operations and growth initiatives.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Business Combination: EM&T completed a business combination with Evolution Metals LLC on January 5, 2026, aiming to build a secure, reliable, and self-sustaining U.S.-aligned supply chain for critical minerals and materials.
  • Capital Management: The company has engaged in several capital management activities, including the issuance of promissory notes to finance transaction costs related to the business combination. These notes are convertible into private units at the sponsor's discretion.
  • Future Outlook: Following the business combination, the company expects to require additional capital to support its operations and growth initiatives. Management is actively pursuing additional sources of capital, including equity and strategic financing arrangements.

Challenges and Risks

  • Limited Operating History: EM&T faces significant challenges due to its limited operating history as a consolidated company following a recent business combination.
  • Operating Losses: The company has incurred operating losses and may struggle to achieve sustained profitability.
  • Growth and Integration Strategy: Success depends on executing its growth and integration strategy, including expanding production capacity and securing reliable sources of feedstock.
  • Market and Operational Risks: The company's future success hinges on its ability to implement growth and go-to-market strategies effectively.
  • Strategic Plan Risks: EM&T's strategic plans are subject to risks, including potential delays in acquisitions, challenges in developing new products, and unforeseen market conditions.
  • Capital Requirements: The company is expected to need additional funds to pursue its growth strategy, which may not be available on favorable terms.
  • Fluctuating Results: EM&T's quarterly net sales and operating results may fluctuate significantly due to various factors, including customer order changes and supply chain disruptions.
  • Market Demand Fluctuations: Market demand fluctuations for battery metals and related products could significantly impact profitability.
  • Industry Growth Dependency: EM&T's success depends on the growth of existing and emerging uses for its products.
  • Industry Consolidation: Industry consolidation may increase competition, potentially reducing EM&T's revenue.
  • Labor and Workforce Risks: The company also faces risks related to labor relations, skilled workforce shortages, and reliance on key personnel.
  • Integration Challenges: Management acknowledges the challenges of integrating recently acquired subsidiaries and the need for effective execution of strategic plans to realize anticipated benefits.
  • Market Risks: EM&T is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in demand and prices for battery metals and rare earth elements.

SEC Filing: Evolution Metals & Technologies Corp. [ EMAT ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Evercore Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Evercore Inc., a leading global independent investment banking advisory firm, has released its 2025 10-K report, showcasing a year of robust financial growth and strategic advancements. The report highlights significant increases in revenue and profitability, driven by strong performance in its Investment Banking & Equities segment, as well as strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing the company's market position and operational efficiency.

Financial Highlights

Evercore Inc. reported impressive financial results for 2025, marked by substantial growth across key metrics:

  • Total Revenues: $3,880.1 million, reflecting a 29% increase from 2024, driven by a significant rise in Advisory Fees and Underwriting Fees.
  • Net Revenues: $3,855.8 million, marking a 29% increase compared to 2024, primarily due to higher Advisory Fees and increased trading volumes.
  • Operating Income: $789.9 million, a 50% increase from 2024, attributed to higher net revenues and effective cost management.
  • Net Income: $640.7 million, representing a 53% increase from 2024, driven by strong performance in the Investment Banking & Equities segment.
  • Net Income Attributable to Evercore Inc.: $591.9 million, a 56% increase from 2024, reflecting improved profitability and revenue growth.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share Attributable to Evercore Inc. Common Shareholders: $14.05, a 55% increase from 2024, indicating enhanced shareholder value.

Business Highlights

Evercore's business performance in 2025 was marked by significant achievements and strategic expansions:

  • Investment Banking & Equities Segment: This segment generated 98% of Evercore's revenues in 2025, with significant contributions from advisory and underwriting transactions. The segment includes strategic advisory, mergers and acquisitions, and equity capital markets services.
  • Geographical Performance: Evercore operates globally with a broad geographic reach, including expansions into Stockholm, Sweden, and Milan, Italy, in 2025. The firm has a strong presence in the United States, with approximately 1,400 employees in Investment Banking & Equities.
  • Sales Units: In 2025, Evercore completed 806 advisory and underwriting client transactions, with 529 transactions generating fees of at least $1 million. The firm also participated in 59 underwriting transactions, acting as a bookrunner in 56 of them.
  • New Product Launches: The firm expanded its capabilities in Private Capital Markets, Financial Sponsors, Technology, Energy, Industrials, and Healthcare sectors through strategic hires and the acquisition of Robey Warshaw in 2025.
  • Human Capital Management: Evercore emphasizes attracting and retaining top talent, promoting 11 Investment Banking Managing Directors to Senior Managing Director in 2025, and announcing further promotions in January 2026.
  • Future Outlook: Evercore plans to continue growing and diversifying its businesses by promoting, recruiting, and acquiring highly qualified professionals, particularly in its Investment Banking & Equities segment. The firm also aims to achieve organic growth and improved profitability in its Investment Management segment.

Strategic Initiatives

Evercore has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its financial flexibility and support growth:

  • Acquisition of Robey Warshaw: This acquisition is expected to strengthen Evercore's advisory capabilities.
  • Reorganization within EMEA Legal Entity Structure: The company reorganized its businesses within the EMEA legal entity structure to optimize operations and improve efficiency.
  • Capital Management: Evercore actively managed its capital through a series of strategic actions, including repurchasing 2,398,458 Class A Shares for $660.6 million to offset the dilutive effect of equity awards and manage its capital structure effectively. Additionally, the company issued $250 million in senior notes (Series K and L) to refinance maturing debt and support general corporate purposes. The company also maintained a revolving credit facility with PNC Bank, increasing its capacity to $225 million to ensure liquidity for working capital and other corporate activities.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Evercore plans to continue its focus on strategic acquisitions and capital management to drive growth and shareholder value. It intends to use proceeds from recent note issuances to repay maturing debt and fund general corporate purposes. The company will also continue to assess its liquidity needs and capital structure to support its strategic objectives, including potential further share repurchases and investments in growth opportunities.

Challenges and Risks

Evercore faces several challenges and risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Competitive Landscape: Evercore operates in a highly competitive financial services industry, facing competition from both large universal banks and independent advisory firms. The company emphasizes its independence from potential conflicts of interest associated with large financial institutions, which it believes enhances its ability to build trusted client relationships. However, the competitive landscape remains intense, particularly in attracting and retaining qualified employees.
  • Market Conditions: Evercore's business is significantly affected by market conditions, which can reduce transaction volumes and values, particularly in its Investment Banking & Equities segment. Economic factors such as rising interest rates, inflation, and geopolitical events can negatively impact financial markets, reducing demand for services and affecting revenue.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces operational risks related to its reliance on senior professionals, whose departure could materially affect its business. Regulatory changes and compliance costs pose additional challenges, as evolving regulations could limit business activities and increase operational costs.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: Cybersecurity risks are also prominent, with potential breaches threatening client data and company reputation.
  • International Operations: The company's international operations expose it to currency fluctuations and regulatory complexities, which could adversely affect financial results.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the volatility in revenue and profits due to the nature of advisory engagements, which are contingent on successful transaction completions. The company is focused on managing growth and profitability, particularly in its Investment Management segment, by effectively managing current operations and selectively expanding wealth management services.

SEC Filing: Evercore Inc. [ EVR ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Douglas Emmett Inc SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Douglas Emmett, Inc., a fully integrated, self-administered, and self-managed real estate investment trust (REIT), has released its 2025 Form 10-K report. The company, which focuses on high-quality office and multifamily properties in Los Angeles and Honolulu, provided a comprehensive overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $1,003,982 million. The increase was primarily due to higher multifamily rental revenues driven by higher occupancy and rental rates, partly offset by lower office occupancy and lower interest income.

Net Income attributable to common stockholders: $16,267 million. The decrease was primarily due to lower office occupancy, higher office expenses, higher interest expense, and lower interest income.

Funds From Operations (FFO): $295,290 million. FFO decreased by $50.2 million, or 14.5%, compared to 2024, primarily due to lower office occupancy, higher office expenses, higher interest expense, and lower interest income, partly offset by higher multifamily rental revenues.

Same Property Net Operating Income (NOI): $608,988 million. The decrease was primarily due to lower office rental revenues due to lower occupancy, partly offset by higher multifamily revenues due to higher occupancy and rental rates.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Douglas Emmett, Inc. is a REIT focusing on high-quality office and multifamily properties in Los Angeles and Honolulu. The company aims to increase its market share in existing submarkets and potentially enter new ones with similar characteristics.

Portfolio Composition: As of December 31, 2025, the Total Portfolio included an 18.0 million square foot office portfolio and 5,445 multifamily apartment units. The office portfolio consists of 70 properties, while the multifamily portfolio includes 15 properties.

Business Strategy: The company employs a focused strategy of concentrating on high-quality office and multifamily properties in supply-constrained submarkets with high barriers to entry. This includes proactive asset and property management to maintain competitive advantages.

Geographical Performance: The company's properties are located in key submarkets of Los Angeles County, California, and Honolulu, Hawaii, with a significant presence in affluent neighborhoods such as Beverly Hills, Brentwood, and Santa Monica.

Segments: Douglas Emmett operates two business segments: office and multifamily. The office segment primarily involves the rental of office space and related services, while the multifamily segment focuses on apartment rentals and associated services.

Development Projects: The company is actively involved in development projects, including the conversion of Studio Plaza in Burbank into a multi-tenant office building and the reconstruction of The Landmark Residences in Los Angeles.

Sustainability Initiatives: Douglas Emmett is committed to environmental sustainability, with efforts to reduce energy consumption, water usage, and waste. The company has implemented energy-efficient technologies and promotes sustainable transportation options.

Community Impact: The company invests in community enhancement projects, such as the development of urban green spaces, to provide amenities to surrounding communities and improve property value.

Human Capital: As of December 31, 2025, Douglas Emmett employed approximately 778 people, focusing on attracting and retaining top talent through competitive compensation, benefits, and a culture of inclusivity and respect.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategy of acquiring substantial market share in its submarkets, leveraging its operational efficiencies and sustainability initiatives to drive long-term growth.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Douglas Emmett, Inc. undertook several strategic initiatives in 2025, including the acquisition of a 17-story office property at 10900 Wilshire Boulevard in Westwood through a joint venture. The company also focused on repositioning projects, such as converting Studio Plaza into a multi-tenant office building and developing The Landmark Residences. These initiatives are aimed at enhancing the company's portfolio and optimizing property use.

Capital Management: In 2025, the company engaged in various capital management activities, including refinancing a $200 million office term loan and closing eight new residential term loans totaling approximately $941.5 million. The company maintained its dividend at $0.19 per share each quarter and did not engage in any equity repurchases. Additionally, the company managed its liquidity by generating $386.9 million in cash from operations and maintaining $340.8 million in cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2025.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Douglas Emmett, Inc. plans to meet its long-term liquidity needs through non-recourse debt, equity securities issuance, and property dispositions. The company aims to continue its development projects, such as the mixed-use community at 10900 Wilshire Boulevard, and manage interest rate risks through swap agreements. The focus remains on strategic acquisitions and developments to drive growth and enhance shareholder value.

Challenges and Risks

Market Risks: Economic and political changes, such as inflation and interest rate fluctuations, could adversely impact operating results and property valuations. The geographic concentration of properties in Los Angeles and Honolulu increases exposure to local economic downturns and regulatory changes.

Operational Risks: The company has a substantial amount of debt, which exposes it to interest rate fluctuation risk and refinancing challenges. The need to maintain, repair, and renovate properties to remain competitive reduces cash flows. Additionally, the reliance on smaller-sized tenants presents greater credit risks.

Regulatory Risks: Changes in property tax laws, such as potential amendments to California's Proposition 13, could significantly increase property taxes. Rent control and stabilization legislation in California and Hawaii may limit the ability to increase rents.

Emerging Risks: The use of AI technologies introduces risks related to data quality and model accuracy, which could impact business operations. Cybersecurity threats pose a risk to IT networks and confidential information.

Environmental Risks: Properties are subject to environmental regulations, and potential liabilities could arise from contamination or mold issues. Climate change may increase the frequency and severity of natural disasters, impacting property values and insurance costs.

Management Strategies: Management is focused on maintaining a disciplined acquisition strategy to grow market share in existing submarkets. The company is also investing in technology and operational efficiencies to mitigate risks associated with tenant retention and property management. Strategies include proactive asset management and leveraging in-house leasing and construction services to reduce vacancy periods and enhance tenant satisfaction.

Interest Rate Risks: The company is exposed to interest rate risks due to its substantial debt, including $1.6 billion in floating rate debt. Prolonged periods of elevated interest rates may negatively impact property valuations and refinancing capabilities. The company employs hedging strategies to mitigate some of these risks but remains vulnerable to market fluctuations.

SEC Filing: Douglas Emmett Inc [ DEI ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Havertys Furniture First Quarter 2026 Cash Dividend
data.description:
ATLANTA, GA / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2026 / Haverty Furniture Companies, Inc. (NYSE:HVT.A) ("Havertys" or the "Company") today announced that its Board of Directors declared a cash dividend to be paid on the outstanding shares of the two classes of $1 par value common stock of the Company at a rate of $0.33 per share on the common stock and $0.31 per share on the Class A common stock. The dividend is payable on March 25, 2026, to stockholders of record at the close of business on March 10, 2026. Havertys has paid a cash dividend each year since 1935.

About Havertys Furniture

Haverty Furniture Companies, Inc. and (NYSE:HVT.A), established in 1885, is a full-service home furnishings retailer with 129 showrooms in 17 states in the Southern and Midwestern regions, providing its customers with a wide selection of quality merchandise in middle to upper-middle price ranges. Additional information is available on the Company's website at www.havertys.com.

Contact:

Havertys 404-443-2900

investor.relations@havertys.com

Tiffany Hinkle

Assistant Vice President, Financial Reporting

SOURCE: Haverty Furniture Companies, Inc.

data.title: Detroit Automakers Raise Concern to White House About Potential New Tariffs — WSJ
data.description:
By Ryan Felton

A trade group representing Detroit's Big Three automakers expressed concern in a letter Friday to President Trump's top trade officials about the potential of new tariffs being imposed, according to a person familiar with the matter.

The American Automotive Policy Council, which represents General Motors, Ford Motor and Jeep-maker Stellantis, asked the White House to exclude from any new levies that might be imposed those vehicles and parts covered by tariffs that remain after the High Court's tariff decision on Friday.

The letter was sent shortly after the Court's ruling was handed down, the person said.

The council stressed that de-stacking current auto tariffs from any new potential levies is essential to sustaining a vibrant and integrated U.S. auto industry, the person said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Transport Canada has certified Gulfstream G500 and G600 jets, document says
data.description:
Transport Canada has certified General Dynamics Gulfstream's G500 and G600 business jets, according to a government document, following a recent spat in which U.S. President Donald Trump criticized the Canadian regulator for taking too long to approve the aircraft.

The head of the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration said earlier this month he expected Canada would soon announce it was certifying some Gulfstream business jets that had been delayed for years. According to a Transport Canada type certificate data sheet dated February 15 that has not been previously reported, the aircraft were approved.

Last month, Trump said in a social media post that the U.S. was planning to decertify Canadian-made Bombardier Global Express business jets and threatened 50% import tariffs on all aircraft made in Canada until the country's regulator certified a number of planes produced by U.S. rival Gulfstream.

The certification of the U.S. jetmaker's G700 and G800 jets is still pending.

The incident raised alarm among aviation experts who argued that planes should be certified for safety reasons and be free from political influence.

Under global aviation rules the country where an aircraft is designed - the U.S. in Gulfstream's case - is responsible for primary certification known as a type certificate, vouching for the design’s safety.

Other countries typically validate the decision of the primary regulator, but have the right to refuse or ask for more data.

data.title: Carnival to Unify U.S. and U.K. Businesses
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Carnival is unifying its U.S. and U.K business.

The cruise company on Friday said it agreed to make its U.K. business a wholly-owned subsidiary of the U.S.-based company

The two businesses have traded separately in the past.

Carnival will also redomicile from Panama, where it is currently located, to Bermuda.

Carnival also plans to issue new CCL shares in accordance with the terms of the arrangement. It will list the new shares of U.S.-based Carnival on the New York Stock Exchange, and delist U.K. Carnival plc ADSs from the NYSE.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Sallie Mae updates investor presentation; reports $7.4B 2025 originations, $729M net income
data.description:
Sallie Mae disclosed an updated investor presentation showing $7.4B in private education loan originations for 2025 and $729M GAAP net income for the year.

Key Highlights:

  • $7.4B private education loan originations in 2025, up 6% vs. 2024; Q4 originations $1.0B, up 4% year‑over‑year.
  • $729M GAAP net income for full‑year 2025; Q4 net income attributable to common stock $229M and GAAP diluted EPS $1.12.
  • Sold $4.95B of private education loans in 2025 (including $4.5B principal + $422M capitalized interest) to manage balance sheet.
  • Returned capital via repurchases: 12.8M shares repurchased in 2025 for $373M; new $500M 24‑month 2026 repurchase program announced.
  • Strong capital and funding metrics: 12.4% total risk‑based capital ratio, CET1 11.1%, deposits $21.1B, cost of funds 4.14% in Q4 2025.

Original SEC Filing: SLM Corp [ SLM ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026
